title: mentha aquatica f. hirsuta
intro: Mentha aquatica f. hirsuta, also known as hairy watermint, is a variant of the peppermint plant characterized by its hairy leaves. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and relieving nausea, due to its high menthol content. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica f. hirsuta include reducing inflammation, easing respiratory issues, and promoting digestion, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
